svg.title: Time-Ordered Products and Hollands-Wald Renormalisation on Curved Spacetimes
svg.desc: A schematic placeholder diagram for time-ordered products of Wick polynomials on a globally hyperbolic curved spacetime. Two interaction vertices x and y are placed on a curved spacetime sheet, each carrying a Wick polynomial normal-ordered phi to the fourth. The time-ordered product T of the two Wick polynomials is a distribution on M times M, defined off the total diagonal by causal factorisation in terms of lower-order time-ordered products via the Bogoliubov recursion, and extended across the diagonal by the Epstein-Glaser inductive construction adapted to curved spacetime by Brunetti-Fredenhagen 2000 and Hollands-Wald 2002 via the Hörmander scaling-degree theorem and Hadamard parametrix subtraction. The renormalisation freedom is a finite-dimensional family of local covariant counterterms classified by Hollands-Wald 2003 as polynomials in the curvature invariants of the appropriate scaling dimension; at dimension 4 in four spacetime dimensions, the counterterm basis is the Riemann-squared, Ricci-squared, scalar-curvature-squared, and box-of-scalar-curvature monomials. The eight Hollands-Wald axioms — locality / causality, general covariance under isometric embeddings, microlocal spectrum condition, scaling, Leibniz, unitarity, smooth metric dependence, and the principle of perturbative agreement — characterise the time-ordered products uniquely up to this counterterm freedom.
svg.text: Inductive Bogoliubov recursion off the diagonal; extension across the diagonal by Hörmander scaling-degree.
freedom: c_1 R^2 + c_2 R_munu R^munu + c_3 R_munurhsigma R^munurhsigma + c_4 box R (dim 4)
Hollands-Wald axioms: causality, covariance, microlocal-spectrum, scaling, Leibniz, unitarity, smooth-metric, perturbative agreement.
Epstein-Glaser 1973 Ann. IHP 19 211; Hollands-Wald 2001/2002/2003 Comm. Math. Phys. 223/231/237.
